title: Author canonical pheno-flags Rust crate (synchronous feature-flag set with env-var loading)

commands: cargo test -p pheno-flags (8 integration + 5 doctest = 13/13 pass)
verification_result.notes: All 8 spec'd integration tests pass (new_flagset_is_empty, with_sets_value, is_enabled_returns_true_for_set_key, is_enabled_returns_false_for_unknown_key, from_env_parses_truthy_values, from_env_parses_falsy_values, from_env_rejects_invalid_value, snapshot_returns_sorted_keys). Verification was performed in the l3-56 worktree directly (no /tmp shim) because the spec says the crate is standalone via an empty [workspace] table, so `cargo test -p pheno-flags` works from inside the crate directory with no workspace coupling. The crate is standalone (NOT a member of the root Cargo.toml [workspace.members]) to match the L3-#46 pheno-errors / L3-#47 pheno-tracing / L3-#57 pheno-plugin convention. Rust edition 2021, rust-version 1.82 (matches root clippy.toml msrv).

summary: Authored the canonical pheno-flags standalone crate: a synchronous, in-memory, FFI-free boolean feature-flag set. Storage is std::collections::HashMap<String, bool> for O(1) is_enabled lookups; FlagSet::snapshot() returns a fresh std::collections::BTreeMap<String, bool> for sorted, observability-friendly iteration. Single dep: thiserror. Consumed by Agentora, Conft, AuthKit and other pheno-* services per the V3 DAG consolidation plan.

public_api.FlagSet::with: self, key: &str, value: bool -> Self - builder; last write wins, chainable
public_api.FlagSet::from_env: prefix: &str -> Result<Self, FlagError> - scans std::env, consumes <PREFIX>_<KEY> vars; atomic (no partial state on error)
public_api.FlagSet::is_enabled: &self, key: &str -> bool - safe default of false for unknown keys
public_api.FlagSet::snapshot: &self -> BTreeMap<String, bool> - fresh sorted copy (HashMap keys cloned into a BTreeMap for deterministic iteration)
public_api.FlagError::InvalidValue: thiserror variant carrying the offending env var name

env_parsing_contract.anything_else: FlagError::InvalidValue(var_name) - returns Err before any state is built
env_parsing_contract.empty_key: FlagError::InvalidValue(var_name) - <PREFIX>_ with no key is a misconfigured var

storage_choice.internal: HashMap<String, bool> - per L3 #56 spec, O(1) is_enabled lookups
storage_choice.snapshot_output: BTreeMap<String, bool> - per L3 #56 spec, sorted ascending keys
storage_choice.conversion_cost: snapshot() is O(n log n) due to the HashMap -> BTreeMap collect, which is fine for the small feature-flag tables this crate is designed to hold

test_coverage: doctest: lib.rs line 14 (FlagSet::new + with + is_enabled quickstart)
test_isolation: Tests that mutate std::env (from_env_*) acquire a process-wide ENV_LOCK (a static Mutex<()>) so parallel test threads cannot race on PHENO_FLAGS_TEST_* variables. The lock uses Mutex::lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) so a previously-poisoned mutex (from a panic in another env-mutating test) does not cascade into this one. The same pattern is used by L3-#48 pheno-config and L3-#57 pheno-plugin.
ffi_free: No extern "C", no uniffi, no cbindgen, no unsafe. Public API is purely safe Rust.
async_free: No tokio, no async-std, no async-trait. All methods are synchronous and &self / self only.
network_free: No reqwest, no hyper, no sockets. Only std::env::vars() and the in-memory HashMap.
consolidation_targets: Agentora (will replace ad-hoc std::env::var("AGENTORA_X") == Ok("1") checks with pheno_flags::FlagSet::from_env("AGENTORA").is_enabled("X") at handler dispatch)
AuthKit (will use pheno_flags::FlagSet::from_env("AUTHKIT").is_enabled("OAUTH_DEV") for OAuth provider toggles)
downstream_consumers: L5 agents across Agentora, Conft, AuthKit, and other pheno-* services will replace ad-hoc std::env::var("FOO") == Ok("1") checks with pheno_flags::FlagSet lookups
notes: Standalone package via empty [workspace] table in its own Cargo.toml, matching the L3-#46 (pheno-errors) and L3-#57 (pheno-plugin) convention. This intentionally avoids touching the root Cargo.toml [workspace.members] (which is being concurrently modified by other L3 agents) and keeps the new crate's test/build loop independent of the 56-crate root workspace. The spec's two storage types are reconciled by using HashMap for the on-disk struct (O(1) is_enabled) and BTreeMap for the snapshot() return value (sorted iteration), with a single O(n log n) collect on snapshot. The from_env method does a two-pass scan: validate all matching variables first (returning InvalidValue on the first unparseable one), then build the HashMap. This means a partial build never escapes the function on error. The internal parse_bool helper recognizes exactly six strings (three truthy, three falsy) and is case-insensitive. The ENV_LOCK in tests is a plain static Mutex<()> (not once_cell/lazy_static) — minimum-dependency approach, fits the FFI-free/no-async/no-network spirit of the crate itself. The previous attempt at this L3 task used BTreeMap internally and put tests in mod tests inside lib.rs; this redo conforms to the L3-#56 spec verbatim: HashMap<String, bool> internal storage and integration tests in tests/flag_test.rs.
no_touch: Other L3 tasks (L3-#46 pheno-errors, L3-#47 pheno-tracing, L3-#48 pheno-config, L3-#49 pheno-otel, L3-#50 pheno-cli-base, L3-#51 pheno-fastapi-base, L3-#52 pheno-go-ctxkit, L3-#53 pheno-zod-pydantic, L3-#54 pheno-tower-stack, L3-#55 pheno-ssot-template, L3-#57 pheno-plugin)
Root Cargo.toml [workspace.members] (pheno-flags is a standalone package, not a workspace member)
The HFS+ case-insensitive FS Justfile/justfile artifact (left in the working tree, not staged)
focus-plugin-sdk and phenotype-registry (different scope; pheno-flags is the boolean-feature-flag path)
